name: romantasy-planning
label: Romantasy Planning (Idea → Outline + Bibles)
description: Idea-to-outline development, ported from the n8n 'Idea to Book Outline - Romantasy V2' workflow. Starts from the book's romantasy genre guide + the author's concept ({{description}}), runs a diversity-then-judge ideation (4 concept sets -> 3 evaluators -> editor-in-chief), develops the winning concept into a chapter outline, refines it (names, engagement hooks, romantasy market standards), then builds the World Bible, Character Bible, and Production Notes. Produces the four files the romantasy-production pipeline consumes. Run this BEFORE romantasy-production (e.g. as a pipeline sequence). The 4 generators and 3 evaluators run as parallel fan-out groups (a `parallel` group each, each member pinned to the n8n source workflow's model: generators on gemini-3-pro / claude-opus-4.8 / grok-5 / gpt-5.5 (temp ~1.0-1.1), evaluators on gemini-3-pro / claude-sonnet-4.6 / gpt-5.5.1 (0.3), all via OpenRouter); the editor-in-chief is the implicit join that waits for all of them.

parallel.label: Concepts A — Dark & Political
parallel.promptTemplate:
You are an expert romantasy concept developer. Using the romantasy genre guide in your context (tropes, reader expectations, comps) and the author's concept below, generate 5 distinct, commercially viable romantasy story concepts.

AUTHOR CONCEPT / BRAINDUMP:
{{description}}

ANGLE FOR THIS SET: lean dark and angsty, with political-intrigue fantasy stakes (courts, factions, betrayal). Make each of the 5 concepts genuinely different from one another.

Every concept MUST support: Romance.io Level 3-4 spice (open-door, not fade-to-black); the MMC introduced within the first 10% (Chapter 1-2); dual first-person POV (FMC + MMC); Power With dynamics by the HEA (the MMC respects the FMC's autonomy — never Power Over/controlling); Female Gaze (her subjective experience, not objectification); a guaranteed HEA/HFN where they end as equals; a dual narrative engine where the romance and the fantasy stakes are interdependent (one cannot resolve without the other); an ACTIVE FMC who drives the plot; and real, conflict-driving flaws for BOTH leads.

NEVER produce: Power Over dynamics with no path to Power With; a passive FMC; romance as a subplot; a late MMC; Level 1-2 (fade-to-black) spice; Male Gaze; generic premises ('orphan discovers powers'); or AI-cliché names (Elara, Lyra, Aria, Kira, Kaelen, Kael, Seraphina, Luna, Thorne).

For each of the 5 concepts give: Title; Logline (WHO + WHAT + STAKES); Premise (4-5 sentences); FMC (name, archetype, primary flaw, arc, agency); MMC (name, archetype, primary flaw, Power-Over→Power-With arc, book-boyfriend factor); Romance Arc (primary trope, initial dynamic, forced proximity, key beats with approximate placement, spice level + why); Fantasy Stakes (external conflict, magic-system role, ticking clock, how it's interdependent with the romance); Commercial Positioning (2-3 comp titles, unique hook, BookTok appeal, sequel potential). Output only the 5 concepts.

parallel.label: Concepts B — Witty & Magical Collapse
parallel.promptTemplate:
You are an expert romantasy concept developer. Using the romantasy genre guide in your context and the author's concept below, generate 5 distinct romantasy concepts that are DIFFERENT from any generated in earlier steps.

AUTHOR CONCEPT / BRAINDUMP:
{{description}}

ANGLE FOR THIS SET: lean witty and fun (banter-forward voice), with a failing/collapsing magic system as the fantasy stakes. Make each of the 5 genuinely different.

Apply the same non-negotiable romantasy requirements as the rest of this pipeline: Level 3-4 open-door spice; MMC in the first 10%; dual first-person POV; Power With by the HEA; Female Gaze; HEA/HFN as equals; an interdependent dual narrative engine; an active FMC; real flaws for both leads. Avoid Power Over, passive FMCs, romance-as-subplot, late MMCs, fade-to-black, Male Gaze, generic premises, and AI-cliché names (Elara, Lyra, Aria, Kaelen, Seraphina, Luna, Thorne).

For each concept give the same fields: Title; Logline; Premise; FMC; MMC; Romance Arc; Fantasy Stakes; Commercial Positioning (as defined in the earlier ideation step). Output only the 5 concepts.

parallel.label: Concepts C — Epic & War
parallel.promptTemplate:
You are an expert romantasy concept developer. Using the romantasy genre guide in your context and the author's concept below, generate 5 distinct romantasy concepts, DIFFERENT from those generated in earlier steps.

AUTHOR CONCEPT / BRAINDUMP:
{{description}}

ANGLE FOR THIS SET: lean epic and sweeping, with realm-scale war or invasion as the fantasy stakes. Make each of the 5 genuinely different.

Apply the same non-negotiable romantasy requirements (Level 3-4 open-door spice; MMC in the first 10%; dual first-person POV; Power With by the HEA; Female Gaze; HEA/HFN as equals; interdependent dual narrative; active FMC; real flaws for both) and the same prohibitions (no Power Over, passive FMC, romance-as-subplot, late MMC, fade-to-black, Male Gaze, generic premises, or AI-cliché names).

For each concept give the same fields: Title; Logline; Premise; FMC; MMC; Romance Arc; Fantasy Stakes; Commercial Positioning. Output only the 5 concepts.

parallel.label: Concepts D — Intimate & Curse-Bound
parallel.promptTemplate:
You are an expert romantasy concept developer. Using the romantasy genre guide in your context and the author's concept below, generate 5 distinct romantasy concepts, DIFFERENT from those generated in earlier steps.

AUTHOR CONCEPT / BRAINDUMP:
{{description}}

ANGLE FOR THIS SET: lean intimate and tightly character-focused, with a curse, bond, or bargain at the center of the fantasy stakes (small cast, claustrophobic forced proximity). Make each of the 5 genuinely different.

Apply the same non-negotiable romantasy requirements and prohibitions as the rest of this pipeline.

For each concept give the same fields: Title; Logline; Premise; FMC; MMC; Romance Arc; Fantasy Stakes; Commercial Positioning. Output only the 5 concepts.

parallel.label: Evaluate Concepts — Market & Genre Lens
parallel.promptTemplate:
You are an expert romantasy acquisitions editor. Review ALL of the romantasy concepts generated in the four ideation steps above (roughly 20 concepts). Score each on a 1-10 scale across these weighted criteria, then pick a single winner:

1. Author-vision alignment (0.25) — captures the author's concept ({{description}}) without contradiction.
2. Romantasy market requirements (0.30) — romance as the A-plot; Level 3-4 spice; MMC in first 10%; Power With; Female Gaze; HEA; dual narrative engine; active FMC; flaws for both.
3. Premise strength (0.20) — hook, stakes, inherent conflict, differentiation, BookTok appeal.
4. Trope execution (0.15) — genre tropes used with fresh angles, stacked, cliche-free.
5. Character potential (0.15) — FMC motivated/flawed/active; MMC book-boyfriend, flawed, vulnerable, Power-With evolution.
6. World-building integration (0.10) — fantasy enhances romance and creates organic conflict.
7. Commercial viability (0.10) — sequel potential, clear comps, current-market fit.

Weighted score = c1*.25 + c2*.30 + c3*.20 + c4*.15 + c5*.15 + c6*.10 + c7*.10 (one decimal). IMMEDIATELY disqualify any concept with Power Over dynamics, a passive FMC, romance-as-subplot, no HEA path, Male Gaze, Level 1-2 spice, or a late MMC. A valid winner needs overall >= 7.5, market >= 7, vision >= 6, and no disqualifiers.

Weight criteria 2 (market requirements) and 1 (author vision) most heavily in your final pick. Output: a brief scored ranking of the strongest contenders, your single WINNER, why it wins, and then the COMPLETE winning concept reproduced verbatim (all fields).

parallel.label: Evaluate Concepts — Craft & Character Lens
parallel.promptTemplate:
You are an expert romantasy developmental editor. Independently review ALL romantasy concepts generated in the ideation steps above and pick the single strongest, judging primarily on CRAFT and CHARACTER (a second, independent opinion to the market-lens evaluation above — reach your own verdict, do not just echo it).

Weigh most heavily: the strength and specificity of BOTH leads' flaws and arcs; whether those flaws actively drive the plot and produce a credible black moment around the 80% mark; the clarity of the FMC's agency; the MMC's book-boyfriend appeal AND his believable Power-Over→Power-With evolution; the emotional logic of the romance arc across its milestones (meeting → forced proximity → first vulnerability → touch starvation → first intimate scene at 55-65% → declaration → black moment → resolution → HEA); and whether the central trope is executed with a genuinely fresh angle.

Disqualify anything with a passive FMC, perfect/flawless leads, Power Over with no path to Power With, or romance demoted to subplot. Output: your top contenders with craft notes, your single WINNER and why, then the COMPLETE winning concept reproduced verbatim.

parallel.label: Evaluate Concepts — Commercial & BookTok Lens
parallel.promptTemplate:
You are a romantasy marketing-minded acquisitions editor. Independently review ALL romantasy concepts from the ideation steps above and pick the single strongest on COMMERCIAL potential (a third independent opinion — form your own verdict).

Weigh most heavily: the strength and shareability of the hook; BookTok appeal (quotable moments, trope stacking, aesthetic); clarity and credibility of 2-3 comp titles in the current market; freshness vs. overdone premises; and series/sequel potential without leaving book 1 incomplete. A concept must still clear the genre floor (Level 3-4 spice, MMC in first 10%, Power With, Female Gaze, HEA, active FMC) to be eligible.

Output: your top contenders with commercial notes, your single WINNER and why, then the COMPLETE winning concept reproduced verbatim.

steps.label: Select Winning Concept (Editor-in-Chief)
steps.promptTemplate:
You are the Editor-in-Chief of a premier romantasy imprint making the final green-light decision for "{{title}}". Three editors independently evaluated the concepts in the steps above and each named a winner. Review their three selections and make the final binding choice.

First determine consensus: do the three picks point at the SAME underlying concept (match on FMC/MMC names, core premise, primary conflict, world elements — wording will differ)? Classify as Unanimous (all three), Majority (two), or Split (all different).

Consensus carries strong weight: a Unanimous or Majority concept gets priority. To override a consensus pick for a single editor's minority choice you MUST show the minority is SIGNIFICANTLY and objectively superior (a fatal flaw the majority missed, a disqualifying red flag in the consensus pick, or a demonstrably stronger hook/vision fit) — never mere preference. If Split, judge all three equally on market requirements, author-vision fit ({{description}}), commercial potential, character-arc strength, and premise differentiation.

Immediately disqualify any option with Power Over dynamics, a passive FMC, romance-as-subplot, no HEA path, Male Gaze, Level 1-2 spice, or a late MMC — regardless of consensus.

Output: the consensus status; your final SELECTED CONCEPT with a 2-3 paragraph justification (and an explicit override defense if you went against consensus); a romantasy market-fit checklist for the winner; and then the COMPLETE green-lit concept reproduced verbatim (all fields). This green-lit concept is the spine of everything downstream. Output only the requested document — no preamble, no commentary, and no questions to the reader.

steps.label: World-Building Review (Cage Framework)
steps.promptTemplate:
You are a developmental editor specializing in fantasy world-building. Using the green-lit concept selected above, the author's concept ({{description}}), and the world conventions in the romantasy genre guide in your context, review and strengthen this story's world through the CAGE FRAMEWORK — a system for active, conflict-driven worlds. You are diagnosing and recommending here, not yet writing the final bible.

Work the four pillars:
1. THE BAN LIST — what is forbidden in this world, and why? ('In this world, no one can [ACTION] because [REASON].') Hard limits generate conflict and force character creativity.
2. THE PUNISHMENT LADDER — for each ban, a 3-step ladder of escalating consequences (warning → severe penalty → irreversible ruin) so the rules have teeth.
3. THE REWARD MATRIX — what this society values and rewards (the carrot to the stick): which behaviors are encouraged and what they earn.
4. THE DILEMMA GENERATOR — combine the pillars into character-defining choices: 'To reach the goal, the character must [forbidden action] and risk [punishment], OR follow the rules and lose [valuable reward].'

Also confirm the magic system has clear rules, limits, and ON-PAGE COSTS (physical, emotional, moral), and that the world's conflicts are interdependent with the romance. Output structured, actionable recommendations organized by pillar — weaknesses and concrete fixes. Output only the requested document — no preamble, no commentary, and no questions to the reader.

steps.label: World Bible
steps.promptTemplate:
Write the complete WORLD BIBLE for "{{title}}", integrating the Cage-Framework recommendations from the step above into a single coherent reference. Build from the green-lit concept and the romantasy genre guide in your context, plus the author's concept ({{description}}).

Cover: the magic system (rules, limits, on-page costs); the Ban List and its Punishment Ladder; the Reward Matrix; political and social structures and factions; geography and key locations; relevant history and lore; the core realm-level stakes and ticking clock; and how the world's conflicts force the romance forward. Keep it consistent and usable as a production reference. Output only the finished World Bible document (this is a production artifact).

steps.label: Chapter Outline
steps.promptTemplate:
You are an expert romantasy developmental editor. Create a detailed, chapter-by-chapter outline for "{{title}}", transforming the green-lit concept and World Bible above into a commercially viable, agent-ready structure. Use the romantasy genre guide in your context for beats and reader expectations.

Produce EXACTLY {{chapterCount}} chapters (this matches the book's production target), about {{wordsPerChapter}} words each, in alternating dual first-person POV (FMC and MMC, roughly balanced). Honor the non-negotiables: the MMC appears in Chapter 1-2 (first 10%); at least two on-page intimate scenes at roughly 55-65% and 70-90% (Romance.io Level 3-4, never before emotional connection); both leads' flaws drive a black moment around the 80% mark; Power With by the HEA; Female Gaze throughout; a clear HEA/HFN where they end as equals; and a dual narrative engine where romance and fantasy stakes are interdependent.

Structure across three acts (~25% / ~50% / ~25%). For EVERY chapter give: number and working title; POV character; 3-5 key beats; the romance-tension shift; the fantasy-plot movement; and the end-of-chapter hook. After the chapters, add: a one-paragraph synopsis, a Romance Arc Summary (the 9 milestones with chapter placements), a Spice Scene Placement note, a Power-Dynamics Evolution note, and a Character-Flaw Consequences note. Number every chapter. Output only the outline (this is a production artifact).

steps.label: Name & Phonetic Cleanup
steps.promptTemplate:
Review the chapter outline above for character and place names and fix AI-cliche / weak naming, then output the FULL outline with names replaced consistently throughout.

INSTANTLY reject and replace these AI-tell names wherever they appear: Elara, Lyra, Aria, Kira, Lara, Lena, Kaelen, Kael, Jael, Rael, Freya, Ophelia, Seraphina, Luna, Orion, Elias, Adrian, Thorne, Asher. Also fix these phonetic problems: more than ~30% of female names ending in '-a'; rhyming or near-identical names; an excess of K-initial names; and random/unmotivated apostrophes. New names should be pronounceable, phonetically diverse, and consistent with any naming conventions or peoples/races described in the World Bible and the romantasy genre guide in your context (give distinct peoples distinct sound-families). Change names ONLY — preserve every beat, POV, and structural element. Output the complete, name-corrected outline with no commentary.

steps.label: Engagement & Hook Pass
steps.promptTemplate:
You are a reader-retention specialist ("the Hook Doctor"). Audit the outline above for engagement and pacing, then APPLY your fixes and output the full revised outline.

Use the 9 engagement techniques to sharpen weak chapters: 1) Reversal, 2) Surprise, 3) Twist, 4) Sacrifice, 5) Secret Revealed, 6) Backstory Trauma, 7) Betrayal, 8) Hidden Relationships, 9) Desecration. Specifically: rewrite every chapter title into something evocative and curiosity-provoking (no generic labels); rework the opening so the book starts in media res (never on waking up or weather); strengthen end-of-chapter hooks; verify rising romantic tension and the touch-starvation build; and make 3-5 surgical high-impact additions where momentum sags. Preserve the chapter count, POV pattern, and the romance/fantasy beats. Output the complete revised outline only.

steps.label: Romantasy Market-Standards Pass
steps.promptTemplate:
You are a brutally honest NYC romantasy developmental editor. Do a final market-standards pass on the outline above and output the FINAL, production-ready outline.

Verify and fix against current romantasy standards: spice at Romance.io Level 3-4 with scenes correctly placed (around 55-65% and 70-90%, never before emotional connection — L1-2 is the wrong genre, L5 is market-limiting erotica); the MMC introduced inside the first 10%; balanced dual first-person POV; an unmistakable Power With resolution (run the test: does she retain the right to make dangerous choices, or does his protection override her autonomy?); Female Gaze throughout; both leads' flaws causing the ~80% black moment; the chosen trope executed against its checklist (e.g. enemies-to-lovers: real opposed goals, respect before attraction, trust tested at the black moment; fated mates: the bond creates pull but love is chosen and consent is never overridden); and a satisfying HEA/HFN where they end as equals. Keep what already works; fix only what falls short. Output the complete final outline only — this is the production outline the writing pipeline will use.

steps.label: Character Bible
steps.promptTemplate:
Build the CHARACTER BIBLE for "{{title}}" from the final outline, World Bible, and green-lit concept above, plus the romantasy genre guide in your context. Write deep profiles for the FMC, the MMC, and each significant secondary character.

For each character cover: Basic Info (name, age, role, appearance, voice/speech pattern); Core Identity (want vs. need, primary flaw, emotional wound, values); Psychological Depth (fears, contradictions, coping behaviors, behavioral signature); Backstory (the formative history that explains the flaw); Character Arc (start state → end state, the lie they believe → the truth they learn); for the leads, a Romantic Arc (their role in the 9-milestone romance, attraction cues, and — for the MMC — his book-boyfriend factor and Power-Over→Power-With evolution); Relationships (to each major character); and Story Function. Keep every detail consistent with the outline and World Bible. Output only the Character Bible (this is a production artifact).

steps.label: Character Depth & Consistency Pass
steps.promptTemplate:
Audit the Character Bible above for depth and consistency, then output the FULL revised Character Bible with your fixes applied.

Flag and fix: any flaw that is a cute quirk rather than a real, plot-driving weakness; leads who read as too perfect or too passive; an MMC whose arc shows Power Over with no credible path to Power With; emotional logic that doesn't track with the backstory; characters who contradict the outline or World Bible; and any thin secondary character who should either be deepened or merged. Strengthen the FMC's agency and both leads' flaw→black-moment causality. Preserve names and established facts. Output the complete, revised Character Bible only.

steps.label: Production Notes
steps.promptTemplate:
You are preparing supplemental PRODUCTION NOTES so a ghostwriter can draft "{{title}}" without getting stuck. Using the final outline, World Bible, Character Bible above, and the author's concept ({{description}}), fill in the practical gaps the other documents leave open. Do NOT repeat what those documents already cover — capture only what's missing.

Include three sections: (1) World/logistics notes — currency and economy, travel times and distances between key locations, the timeline/calendar and how the plot's events sit on it, technology/magic limits that affect everyday scenes, and any named rules a writer will need mid-scene. (2) Character/continuity notes — secondary-character details and relationships not in the bible, naming of any unnamed but plot-relevant figures, recurring objects/devices and their rules, and physical or status details that must stay consistent. (3) Other production notes — plot devices that must pay off, foreshadowing that must be planted early, and anything fragile that needs careful handling. Output only the Production Notes (this is a production artifact).

steps.label: Compile Production Package
steps.promptTemplate: Produce a short readiness report for "{{title}}" confirming the romantasy production package is complete. List the four production artifacts created by this pipeline — Chapter Outline ({{chapterCount}} chapters), World Bible, Character Bible, and Production Notes — and confirm each is present in the steps above. Flag any gaps or inconsistencies a human should resolve before drafting, and state that the book is ready for the romantasy-production pipeline. Keep it concise.
